title: kokia drynarioides
intro: Kokia drynarioides, also known as the Kokia tree, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the hau tree family (Sapindaceae) that is native to the Hawaiian Islands. It has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and poultices for medicinal use, although its use is now largely limited to research and conservation efforts.
